text: Hey — Patricia just forwarded me the Alvarez file, USCIS wants the I-589 supplement by Friday. You still have the client's declaration draft?
Yeah, it's on the shared drive under Alvarez_2026. I'm in ESOL till 7 tonight but I'll send you the translated version after.
Perfect, no rush. How's the class going this session, still got that big group from El Salvador?
Mostly Honduras this time, 14 students. One guy asked me to write his kid's teacher a note in English lol.
Classic. Hey did you end up rescheduling the Alvarez biometrics appt or is that still Thursday?
Still Thursday 9am at the Fairfax ASC. I'll text her the address again, she got lost last time.
Good call. I'll swing by your office around 5 to grab that declaration then.
Sounds good, I'll leave it on my desk with sticky notes. Gracias Daniel.
Hey - you catch Patricia's email? Hernandez family's I-130 biometrics got bumped to next Friday.
Yeah just saw it. Gonna have to shuffle my ESOL grading time around that now lol
Speaking of shuffling - you still good to swap Thursday sections? Got that CASA volunteer training at 6.
Yeah that works, I'll take your citizenship class Tuesday instead. Bring the naturalization worksheets with you?
Will do. Also Patricia wants the affidavit translated before the biometrics appt - you or me?
I'll knock it out tonight after I get Mum settled, have it on her desk by morning.
Solid, thanks Ian. Owe you a coffee at that Silver Spring place Friday.
Deal. Black drip, none of that fancy stuff ☕
Ian, you catch the walk-in this morning? Sra. Fuentes says her I-601A got RFE'd again.
Yeah saw it. Patricia wants us to resubmit by Friday with the new hardship letter.
Ugh, third time. She's shaken — told her to come back with her husband's medical records.
Good call. I'll draft the cover letter tonight after my ESOL class wraps at Montgomery College.
You still teaching Tuesdays? Respect. How's the group doing with past tense drills lol
Slow but steady jaja. Half of them are learning English while working night shifts.
Same energy as our clients honestly. Ok I'll email you the Fuentes packet by 3.
Perfect, I'll have the letter back to Patricia before I head home to Silver Spring.
hey did you finish the intake packets for Saturday's workshop at St. Camillus?
Almost, printing 40 copies now. Patricia wants the N-400 checklist stapled separately so people stop losing it.
smart. how many volunteer translators we got lined up?
Three so far, still need one for Amharic. Gonna ask around at Casa tonight.
I can check with my neighbor, she's fluent. also heads up, Marta Delgado emailed again about her husband's I-751.
Tell her I'll call after my ESOL class tomorrow, like 4ish. Been swamped grading essays.
will do, good luck with class jajaja your students still stuck on idioms?
Yes 😂 today's was 'bite the bullet.' Anyway send me Marta's number, I'll call her tonight.
